what_it_does: This file is a research note that inventories existing vi-visual-bridge mechanisms (mode-to-colour CSS variables, a MODE_CHROME surface registry, and the slide-format render pipeline with the :::options directive) and maps them to a proposed V-surface / decision / mode system, plus speculative 'beyond' extensions.
observations: Cites server.py ~3874 for body[data-mode] setting --mode-primary and ~12699 for the MODE_CHROME registry.
Lists mode-to-colour mappings: slide_mode gold (#c8943a), document/document_channel_split blue (#4a90d9), graph_mode terracotta (#b85e3e).
Describes a 15-step slide render pipeline where :::options already renders option cards with label, implication, and recommended flags.
Frames modes as projections of one system, with the V acting as a single modes-setter.
Includes a 'beyond' section proposing the V-surface can retarget MAKE at its own mode rows and that decisions are one resolvable type among many.
Ends with an explicit verification-state section distinguishing verified code reads from synthesis/reasoning.
standouts: Mode-to-colour and mode-to-surface already exist in vi-visual-bridge as --mode-primary and the MODE_CHROME registry.
The slide-format decision surface is already live via the :::options directive.
The proposed architecture is a single resolver over a growing type registry, with the surface able to modify its own mode rows.
conventions: Follows a research-first structure with verified, how-it-maps, beyond, seams, and verification-state sections.
Quotes Tim directly to anchor each research question.
Provides code file and line-number references for verifiable claims.
concerns: Heavily mixes verified code observations with speculative extensions; a reader must track the verification-state labels carefully.
Uses project-specific jargon (gallery:verb, DNA, composition, fork, recollection, projection) without defining it locally.
Line-number references may drift as the referenced files change.
The convergence of three palette/composition systems is asserted as a flag but not verified in this file.
notes: A research-first bridge document that tries to show the requested V-surface already has working precedents in vi-visual-bridge and can be elevated into a type-row resolver.
questions: What is the exact definition of 'the V' in this context?
How do the proposed mode-type, option-type, and decision-type schemas relate to the existing MODE_CHROME registry?
What are gallery:verb, DNA, composition, fork, recollection, and projection in concrete terms?
Are the cited server.py line numbers still current?
How would the self-modifying 'add a mode' loop avoid circularity or unsafe live edits?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to do research from all sides and beyond, feeding a lead synthesis. The file does exactly that: it verifies existing mechanisms, maps them to the ask, and adds speculative extensions.
apparent_themes: Modes as projections rather than separate UIs.
intention_signals: The file appears to be arguing that the requested V-surface, decision cards, and mode-driven colour/surface composition are not green-field features but can be built by promoting existing vi-visual-bridge patterns into typed, resolvable rows.
novelty: It frames the V-surface as a self-modifying type-row resolver and treats mode colour as a semantic, state-resolved meaning-field rather than a fixed decorative token.
contribution: This file contributes a catalog of existing precedents (mode-primary CSS variable, MODE_CHROME registry, slide pipeline, :::options directive), a modes-as-projections framing, and speculative ideas (self-building surface, generic type-row resolver, semantic colour) for a fused system to reconcile.
summary_for_embedding: A research note mapping existing vi-visual-bridge mode-colour, mode-surface, and slide-render mechanisms to a proposed V-surface type-row resolver with speculative self-building extensions.
intention_for_embedding: To verify and synthesize how existing bridge code can satisfy a decision/mode-driven V-surface ask while pushing beyond into a self-modifying type-row architecture.
suggested_fields: code_reference with line numbers and file
